title: Fix-first: mutual exclusion still fails in the PAST-CEILING takeover path (scenario f)
action: v1.9.0's exact-tag CI is RED on Linux (run 29857980871, verify(22), 2-core): scripts/test_store_lock.mjs scenario (f) — '(f) run recorded 3 mutual-exclusion violation(s) — the lock did not exclude'. Scenario (f) races racers at a LIVE lock that is past HARD_STALE_MS (the ceiling takeover), which is a DIFFERENT code path from scenario (c) (the stale-heartbeat takeover) that rel180-4 fixed in commit a0111c0. That fix split judge/claim and verified the renamed-away body still matched pid+token+ts of the snapshot it judged; the ceiling path evidently did not get the same treatment, or gets it in a way that still admits two winners. ROOT-CAUSE THE CLASS, not the instance: audit EVERY path in lib/lock.mjs that can transfer ownership of a held lock (stale-heartbeat takeover, past-ceiling takeover, sweep/reclaim, any force/override), and give them all the same atomic judge-then-claim-with-identity-verification discipline — a rename wins a PATH, never a FILE, so any code that acts on an earlier judgement must re-verify identity of what it actually consumed and undo on mismatch (learning 20260722-lock-takeover-verifies-what-it-deletes). RED-FIRST: force the (f) interleaving deterministically (the same seam technique rel180-4 used for (c) — do NOT chase it with load) so it fails 10/10 against current code; also add a forced test for any OTHER ownership-transfer path the audit finds unprotected. Record --passed false, then fix. Prove: forced tests 10/10; test_store_lock 10/10 plain AND 10/10 under `taskset -c 0,1`; full run_verify green 3 consecutive times. Tag v1.9.0 will be re-pointed to the fix commit.

truths: every ownership-transfer path in lock.mjs verifies the identity of what it consumed before acting on an earlier judgement
the (f) past-ceiling interleaving is forced deterministically and fails 10/10 before the fix, passes 10/10 after
prohibitions: Never weaken a mutual-exclusion assertion or make the test tolerant of violations
Do not fix only scenario (f) — audit and cover every ownership-transfer path

trace.outcome: Takeover re-verifies identity BEFORE the rename; an actively-held lock is never briefly vacated for a third racer
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":["test_store_lock (a)-(g) incl. rel180-4 forced-interleaving (g)"],"tests_added":["scenario (g2): a THIRD racer exploits the vacancy the takeover rename creates; the existing 2-racer (g) structurally could not catch it"],"red_failure_evidence":"Against the pre-fix lock.mjs, forced scenario (g2) fails 10 of 10 rounds: the takeover renames the lock away before checking identity, the lock path is genuinely empty on disk for that instant, and a third racer's ordinary tryAcquire wins the vacancy and enters its critical section while the original holder is still inside its own — two holders at once, which is precisely the mutual-exclusion violation CI reported as scenario (f).","verification_run":"Audit: (c) and (f) are the SAME path (performTakeoverClaim, shared by sync/async takeover and by both dead-pid and past-ceiling reasons) — one unprotected path, not two. rel180-4 verified identity AFTER the rename, preventing permanent loss of a live holder's lock but not the double entry. Fix re-verifies pid+token+ts immediately BEFORE the rename in both paths; the post-rename check stays as a second layer. Orchestrator re-verified: store_lock 5/5 plain + 3/3 under taskset -c 0,1; mirror byte-identical; full chain green 3 consecutive runs (58 suites)."}
trace.verify_output: PASS test_store_lock: isPidAlive() unit checks green; (a) 6x12 locked racers zero violations/exact count; (b) unguarded control demonstrated loss/overlap; (c) 6x12 racers survived a pre-seeded stale lock with zero violations/exact count; (d) LOCK_BUSY refusal named the real holder after the real retry budget; (e) a 45s-backdated LIVE-pid lock was NOT stolen (LOCK_BUSY refusal); (f) 6x12 racers took over a live-but-past-HARD_STALE_MS-ceiling lock anyway, zero violations/exact count; (g) 10/10 forced-interleaving stale-takeover races held mutual exclusion deterministically; (g2) 10/10 forced third-racer vacancy-exploit attempts held mutual exclusion deterministically; (h) simulated transient/permanent EPERM on acquire/release/takeover-rename retried and surfaced correctly, bounded.
